data.name: severity.info
data.comment: [scaffold-pilot — replace in Plan 6] severity.info in blocks-maintenance context — status badge for informational maintenance messages (e.g., RFQ sent, vendor confirmed, work order opened). Short noun; sentence case.

data.name: severity.warning
data.comment: [scaffold-pilot — replace in Plan 6] severity.warning in blocks-maintenance context — status badge for non-blocking maintenance concerns (e.g., quote nearing expiry, vendor SLA approaching). Short noun; sentence case.

data.name: severity.error
data.comment: [scaffold-pilot — replace in Plan 6] severity.error in blocks-maintenance context — status badge for blocking maintenance failures (e.g., RFQ broadcast rejected, work-order state-transition invalid). Short noun; sentence case.

data.name: severity.critical
data.comment: [scaffold-pilot — replace in Plan 6] severity.critical in blocks-maintenance context — status badge for tenant-impacting maintenance failures (e.g., emergency work order missed SLA). Pairs with high-attention visual styling.

data.name: action.save
data.comment: [scaffold-pilot — replace in Plan 6] action.save in blocks-maintenance context — pilot string for the cluster-B cascade proof. UI button or status label when persisting a maintenance record (vendor, request, RFQ, quote, work order). Imperative; sentence case. Trailing U+2026 ellipsis indicates in-progress.

data.name: action.cancel
data.comment: [scaffold-pilot — replace in Plan 6] action.cancel in blocks-maintenance context — dismiss/abort button on maintenance forms and dialogs. Imperative; sentence case.

data.name: action.retry
data.comment: [scaffold-pilot — replace in Plan 6] action.retry in blocks-maintenance context — retry button after an RFQ broadcast, save, or work-order transition failure. Imperative; sentence case.

data.name: state.loading
data.comment: [scaffold-pilot — replace in Plan 6] state.loading in blocks-maintenance context — empty-state placeholder while fetching maintenance data. Trailing U+2026 ellipsis (single character); translators preserve the convention.
